{
 "cells": [
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "c6ac3c28-eb82-409d-b21c-0baff26c20bd",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# Final Project Conclusion\n",
    "\n",
    "This project developed a coherent and progressively structured computational study of optical interference phenoemena, bridging analytical wave optics and numerical simulation.\n",
    "\n",
    "Starting from simple two-wave interference, the study advanced toward increasingly complex systems incluiding thin films,Fabry-Pérot resonators, and ultimately arbutrary multilayer stacks modeled through the Transfer Matrix Method.\n",
    "\n",
    "The numerical implementation was rigorously validated by:\n",
    "\n",
    "- Explicit verification of enerfy conservation.\n",
    "- Analytical comparison of Bragg mirror stop-band width.\n",
    "- Phyisical consistency checks at each stage.\n",
    "\n",
    "The final model successfully reproduces:\n",
    "\n",
    "- Resonant cavity behavior\n",
    "- Constructive and destructive interferences\n",
    "- Photonic band gap formation in periodic structures\n",
    "\n",
    "This work stablishes a solid foundation for future exploration of:\n",
    "\n",
    "- Photonic crystals\n",
    "- Optical coatings\n",
    "- Nanophotonics\n",
    "- Integrated photonic devices\n",
    "\n",
    "The project demonstrates the power of combining theorical electromagnetism with computational physics to analyze and desing complex optical systems."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": null,
   "id": "299a908a-45f8-4a65-92c9-6fc728a97705",
   "metadata": {},

  }
